notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Midwest is wide-open horizon, continental winter (DRIER and WINDIER than Great Lakes), with agricultural infrastructure as the defining built environment and the highest per-capita watercraft density in the system. Year-End cells can reference harvest's tail into early winter (grain trucks, corn stubble). High-confidence climate-and-landscape is operative; sensory mood is creative reference.

winter.retail_moment: Big Finish (Dec), Black Friday (Nov), Start Something New (Jan)
winter.weather: cold and continental; northern Plains (MN, ND, SD) subzero overnights routinely; single-digit or below-zero daytime highs in worst stretches; WIND defines (windchill cuts harder than thermometer); snow accumulates, lake-effect absent except eastern MN edge; Plains snow dry, drifts heavily, blows across roads; southern Midwest (MO, KS) milder with ice-storm risk
winter.light: low-angle sun, big-sky horizon; short days; snow reflects light brightly; clear cold days common; storm fronts move through fast
winter.landscape: wide horizon; agricultural infrastructure visible (silos, grain elevators, machine sheds, corn stubble, livestock barns); small towns with main streets and water towers; metros (Minneapolis-St. Paul, KC, Omaha, Des Moines) urban-suburban; snow on roofs, plowed county roads, drifts at fence lines
winter.activity_rhythm: ice fishing real and serious (MN especially); snowmobile culture intense; late deer, waterfowl, upland-bird hunting; indoor equipment maintenance for farmers; trucks plow, haul, support ranching/farming
winter.cgi_footage_implication: Northern Midwest winter is colder and more wind-exposed than GL; big sky, big drift patterns, less tree cover; trucks carry windburn and ice as much as snow.

spring.retail_moment: Declaration of Deals (Mar-Apr), 4x4 Day, Truck Month
spring.weather: late thaw in north (April), earlier in south; mud season real; May is planting season (defining agricultural moment); spring storms severe (Plains tornado season peaks May-June); hail risk; snow possible through April in northern Plains
spring.light: lengthening fast, clearer than winter, late golden hour across open horizon; storm-light dramatic
spring.landscape: fields turning bare to plowed to planted in weeks; new green emerging; farm equipment moving (tractors, planters, sprayers); outdoor construction starting; cattle to pasture
spring.activity_rhythm: planting season row-crop ag (corn, soybeans, wheat) defining; calving and lambing; spring fishing opens; trucks support agricultural and construction work

summer.retail_moment: Make This The Summer Event, Summer of Jeep
summer.weather: hot and humid eastern Midwest, hot and dry western Plains; daily highs 80s-90s, occasional 100s; severe-weather peak (tornado, hail, derecho) through July; lake culture central in MN and across small-lake states
summer.light: long days, big golden hour, storm-light dramatic; wide horizon shows weather coming hours ahead
summer.landscape: corn knee-high by July, tasseled by August; pastures green; lakes busy (MN cabin culture, Lake of the Ozarks, KS reservoirs, IA recreational lakes); state and county fairs
summer.activity_rhythm: lake-and-cabin rhythm MN/WI/IA; wakeboard and ski culture; small-lake boating (highest per-capita watercraft density in system); cookouts, family reunions; farming concentrated in growing season

fall.weather: crisp, clear; northern Midwest cools fast October; first frost mid-Oct; HARVEST season Sept-Nov is the defining rhythm; Indian summer days possible into November; first snow late Nov in north
fall.light: bright, clear, color in deciduous (maples, cottonwoods, oaks) where present; harvest dust in air; big late-golden-hour
fall.landscape: combine harvest across the landscape (corn Sept-Oct, soybeans Oct, wheat earlier); grain trucks running to elevators; equipment in fields; pumpkin patches; hunting blinds going up
fall.activity_rhythm: harvest is the defining moment (16-hour days, equipment running, grain moving); Big Ten Saturdays, NFL Sundays, Friday lights; deer season opens; waterfowl strong in Mississippi flyway states
